topTargets.name: Emmett Johnson
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #13 Tier 2 (KC), RSP DoT 84.6 (#8 — RSP loves him as Harold Fannin trajectory comp), no ADP top-30 (market lag). Medium-affinity GM weights the RSP+Consensus alignment: cross-board Tier-2 RB at thinnest position (RB:3) with elite KC landing. The ADP gap means he could even fall, making 1.11 a fair value rather than a reach.

topTargets.name: Omar Cooper Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #12 Tier 2 (NYJ), RSP DoT 83.9 (#15), ADP 17.20 (#27) — cleanest triple-board Tier-2 player likely on the board at 1.11. NYJ landing pairs with Sadiq buzz. Even with WR:7, a Deebo-clone outside-WR profile fits a roster trying to harvest upside on its only top-3-round pick.

topTargets.name: Mike Washington Jr.
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #11 Tier 2 (LV) — the literal Consensus rank match for slot 1.11. RSP cooler at DoT 82.5 (#28) but ADP 24.09 (#42) is the floor. Medium-affinity GM still anchors 50% to Consensus and the RB:3 need pushes them past the RSP/ADP lag. The exact-rank-meets-slot logic is the cleanest pure-Consensus floor.

topTargets.name: Chris Brazzell II
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #15 Tier 2 (CAR), RSP DoT 84.2 (#12 — Pickens comp), ADP 26.47 (#48). Three-board agreement in Tier-2/late-Tier-2 range. Slight reach at 1.11 but the RSP love and high-ceiling profile fit a one-pick-only team that needs upside, not floor.

topTargets.name: Nicholas Singleton
topTargets.reasoning: Consensus #17 Tier 2 (TEN), RSP DoT 84.1 (#14), ADP 25.83 (#46). Solid cross-board signal at thinnest position. A reach at 1.11 by Consensus rank, but RB:3 need plus the RSP/ADP confirmation makes him a viable backup plan if Johnson and Washington Jr. are both gone.

capPosture: Tight at $39.9M used / $5.1M space — the 1.11 rookie almost certainly goes to taxi (50% base hit) to preserve flex for in-season moves; only 1 expiring contract means little natural relief is coming.

wildcard.name: Eli Stowers
wildcard.reasoning: Consensus #21 Tier 3 (PHI), RSP DoT 84.4 (#11 — RSP top-15 with strong Move-TE upside), ADP 21.25 (#35). TE:5 is already crowded so this is unlikely, but a medium-RSP GM could rationalize it as BPA + Eagles offense + RSP edge. The crowded TE room is the primary blocker.

summary: DMOC owns only 1.11 — no R2 or R3 picks — so this is a one-shot draft and they cannot afford to miss. With RB:3 the thinnest position, the GM targets the Tier-2 RB cluster (Emmett Johnson primary on RSP+Consensus alignment, Washington Jr. as the rank-match Consensus floor) while keeping Cooper Jr. as the WR fallback if both RBs are gone. Cap reality forces the rookie to taxi to keep $5.1M of flex intact through the season.
